commit.message:
feat: add per-identity API rate limiting and tighten registration limits (#1264)

Add ApiRateLimiter with sliding-window per-IP tracking for /api/* routes:
- GET (read) operations: 300 requests/minute per identity
- POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (write) operations: 60 requests/minute per identity
- Lock-free hot path using Interlocked CAS operations
- Automatic stale bucket pruning every 2 minutes

Rate limit check runs centrally in RequestHandler after session context
is built but before route dispatch, returning 429 with Retry-After
header and proper ApiErrorWriter JSON for AJAX callers.

Also:
- Tighten registration per-IP limit from 10 to 3 attempts per window
- Add RequestsInFlight gauge to Prometheus /metrics/prometheus endpoint
- Fix upstream StubMetricsTracker (add EnterRequest/LeaveRequest +
  RequestsInFlight parameter)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: wain
repository.description: Rust/Go interface to Mesa, Vulkan

commit.message:
perf: fix ARM64 router regression — branchless AsciiLower, batched metrics, vectorised match

Three hot-path fixes for NativeAOT on ARM64:

1. RecordDispatch: replace 3x Interlocked per call with thread-local
   accumulators flushed every 64 calls. ARM LL/SC (LDAXR/STLXR) is
   2-3x costlier than x86 LOCK prefix; batching amortises by 64x.

2. AsciiLower: replace dual-branch ternary (b >= 'A' && b <= 'Z')
   with SUB/SUB/ASR/AND/ORR arithmetic mask. NativeAOT's ARM64 backend
   emits real conditional branches for ternaries (no JIT to lower to
   CSEL), making the old code unpredictable for mixed-case slugs.

3. TryResolve: pre-lowercase slug once via AsciiLowerSpan, then use
   SequenceEqual for all bucket comparisons. The BCL SequenceEqual
   compiles to AdvSimd-accelerated memcmp under NativeAOT on ARM64.
   Also moves Stopwatch.GetTimestamp() after the empty-slug guard to
   skip the CNTVCT_EL0 system-register read on the fast path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: setup modal only shows to OWNER on first visit; fix missing setupCompletedAt in GET response

Two root causes:
1. GET /api/tenant/[orgId] never returned setupCompletedAt, so the modal always
   showed (data?.setupCompletedAt was always undefined = falsy = done:false)
2. Modal showed to ALL members but PATCH /api/tenant/[orgId]/setup requires OWNER,
   so non-owners would see the modal and get 403 on submit

Fixes:
- GET /api/tenant/[orgId] now returns setupCompletedAt and userRole fields
  (system admins get synthetic OWNER role, others get their membership role)
- TenantLayout tracks isOwner from the API response; modal only renders when
  setupCompletedAt is null AND userRole === 'OWNER'
- Non-owners and non-system-admins never see the modal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: PII redaction in log output (#1272)

- Add LogRedactor with span-based char scanning (no regex on hot path)
- Field-level masking: IPs (first octet only), emails (2-char local + domain)
- Free-text email scanner: single-pass O(n), zero alloc when no '@' found
- DiskBufferedLogger.RedactPII flag applied in FormatJsonEntry on structured fields
- Enabled by default via Logging.RedactPII config key (opt-out with false)
- Fix 4 SSO log statements that explicitly embedded email addresses
- Config validation for Logging.RedactPII boolean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add hypervisor health check before each benchmark run

Collect load average, memory, swap, and process count from the
hypervisor before starting benchmarks on each VM. Abort the play
if the hypervisor is overloaded (load/CPU > 2.0, free memory
< 1024 MB, or swap > 90% used with low memory). Thresholds are
configurable via extra vars. The swap check is only enforced when
available memory is also low, avoiding false positives from stale
cold pages in swap.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add keep-alive script to prevent Azure Function cold starts (#14)

Adds scripts/keep-alive.sh — a lightweight cron-friendly script that
pings the /api/health endpoint every 15 minutes to keep the Azure
Function warm on the free tier.

- Silent curl with 10s timeout; logs timestamp + HTTP status
- Writes to ~/.local/share/aalokdeep-keepalive/keep-alive.log
- Self-rotating log (capped at 500 lines)
- Setup instructions included as header comments
- No deployment workflows are affected (scripts/ is not in any SWA path filter)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add ESLint, Jest, Node 24, and Nx plugin tooling

- Added @nx/jest plugin (auto-infers test target from jest.config.*)

- Root .eslintrc.js with eslint:recommended + @typescript-eslint/recommended

## Node version

- Documented Node 24 / nvm requirement
- Documented preference for Nx first-party plugins over manual config

  tsconfig.spec.json

commit.message:
Fix block ID insertion position: use char count not byte len

The rope insert position was computed from String::len() (byte
length) instead of char count, causing IDs to be inserted at
the wrong position — typically the start of the next line
instead of the end of the current line.

Fix: walk backwards through the line's chars to find the content
end, counting in char units that match the rope's coordinate
system.

Add test: test_save_block_ids_multiline verifies correct placement
across paragraph boundaries with newlines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add bulk block ID assignment after first index

assign_block_ids_bulk() runs once after the first IndexComplete
where files_changed > 0. It reads all .md files from disk, parses
them, generates IDs for blocks without them, and writes back via
the DiskWriter channel. Files with all IDs present are skipped.

Shows notification: 'Assigned block IDs to N files'.

The block_ids_bulk_done flag ensures this runs only once per
session — subsequent index completions (incremental) skip it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Real terminal with in-panel input() + fix code block width

TERMINAL INPUT (Python):
- Python now runs in a Web Worker (public/py-worker.js)
- Worker uses SharedArrayBuffer + Atomics.wait() for blocking input()
  → Worker calls input() → sends 'input_request' message → blocks at Atomics.wait()
  → Panel shows an inline terminal input row at the bottom
  → User types and presses Enter → value written to SAB → Atomics.notify()
  → Worker unblocks, returns value to Python, execution continues
- No more browser prompt() dialog — input happens inline in the terminal
- COI service worker (public/coi-serviceworker.js) adds COOP/COEP headers
  to all responses so SharedArrayBuffer is available (required for Atomics.wait)
- On first visit the page reloads once (SW activates), then works permanently
- Fallback: if crossOriginIsolated=false (old SW not yet active), uses
  main-thread Pyodide with prompt() dialog

TERMINAL UX:
- Monospace terminal output area with real-time stdout/stderr streaming
- Stdout: white. Stderr: red. Echoed input: dimmed
- When input() is called, an input line appears at the bottom of the terminal
  — user types and presses Enter, text echoes into terminal, Python continues
- Matplotlib plots render as images below text output
- Running spinner at bottom while executing

CODE BLOCK WIDTH:
- Added width:100% to .chat-bubble-assistant so code blocks stretch to
  the full width of the chat area (was shrink-wrapping to text content)

GODBOLT: Java public class fix (strips 'public' keyword) verified working

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat/dotenvx-encryption (#54)

* feat(dotenvx): Phase 1 - Setup dotenvx infrastructure

- Install @dotenvx/dotenvx package
- Update .gitignore to exclude .env.vault.keys and .env.vault.local.keys
- Update package.json scripts to use 'dotenvx run --' for dev/build/test/start
- Add dotenvx-specific scripts: encrypt, decrypt, keys:generate
- Create .env.example template with all required environment variables

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(dotenvx): Phase 2 - Environment encryption and configuration

- Update next.config.ts to initialize dotenvx/config
- Encrypt .env.local secrets to create .env.vault
- Generate .env.vault.keys for decryption (gitignored)
- Restore plaintext .env.local for development
- Verify encryption/decryption works correctly

Secrets encrypted:
- DATABASE_URL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
- CLERK_SECRET_KEY
- SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION

The .env.vault.keys file contains the decryption key and should never be committed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(dotenvx): Phase 3 - Testing and verification

Verified:
✅ pnpm build succeeds with dotenvx integration
✅ pnpm dev starts successfully with encrypted envs
✅ dotenvx properly injects environment variables
✅ TypeScript compilation passes
✅ Biome linting passes
✅ All non-database tests pass (test failures pre-existing, DB timeout unrelated)

Environment loading:
- dotenvx run -- properly decrypts and injects vars
- .env.local works for development
- .env.vault ready for production use
- .env.vault.keys kept secure (gitignored)

No regressions introduced by dotenvx integration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add developer-focused documentation:

- DOTENVX_SETUP_GUIDE.md: Complete guide for setup, encryption, team sharing
  * Quick start for new developers
  * Team member key sharing procedures
  * Common tasks (encrypt, decrypt, update secrets)
  * Troubleshooting section
  * Security best practices
  * Scripts reference

- Update README.md with security section
  * Link to setup guide
  * Quick 3-step setup instructions
  * Environment variables setup instructions
  * Reference to encryption guide for production

Documentation covers:
✅ Initial project setup with .env.local
✅ Encrypted vault (.env.vault) for production
✅ Team key management and sharing
✅ Encryption/decryption workflows
✅ Security best practices
✅ Troubleshooting common issues

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(docs): Move dotenvx guide to docs directory and update references

- Move DOTENVX_SETUP_GUIDE.md from generated/docs-copilot/ to docs/dotenvx-setup-guide.md
- Update docs/README.md to include dotenvx guide in documentation index
  * Add as first entry in Core Documentation Files section
  * Include purpose, size, key sections, and usage guidelines
  * Link in Related Documentation section
- Update README.md to reference new location
  * Change generated/docs-copilot/DOTENVX_SETUP_GUIDE.md → docs/dotenvx-setup-guide.md
  * Keep all content and functionality the same
- Remove empty generated/docs-copilot directory

This consolidates all project documentation in the docs/ directory for better organization and discoverability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gitignore): Add SQLite journal and transaction log files

- Add /prisma/dev.db-journal to .gitignore
- Add /prisma/dev.db-wal to .gitignore (Write-Ahead Log)
- Add /prisma/dev.db-shm to .gitignore (Shared memory for WAL)
- Remove prisma/dev.db-journal from git tracking

SQLite automatically creates journal files during transactions for:
- Rollback capability
- Write-ahead logging (WAL mode)
- Data integrity guarantees

These files are temporary, machine-specific, and should never be
committed to version control. They can cause merge conflicts and
add unnecessary repository bloat.

The .db-journal file (29 KB) was previously tracked in git and is
now properly excluded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add --help flag support to all demo binaries

Implements audit finding: 'Demo binaries lack --help documentation'

- Created internal/demo/flags.go with shared helpers:
  * CheckHelpFlag() - checks for --help and exits with usage
  * PrintUsageAndExit() - displays formatted usage information
  * FormatExample() - formats usage examples with alignment

- Added --help support to all 16 demonstration binaries:
  * amd-triangle-demo, auto-render-demo, clipboard-demo
  * decorations-demo, dmabuf-demo, double-buffer-demo
  * gen-atlas, gpu-display-demo, gpu-triangle-demo
  * perf-demo, shader-test, wayland-demo
  * widget-demo, x11-demo, x11-dmabuf-demo

- All demos now provide consistent help output with:
  * Program name and description
  * Usage examples with comments
  * Link to project repository

Verified with: for bin in bin/*-demo; do $bin --help | grep -q 'Usage:'; done
All tests pass: make test-go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: account keys now persisted to database during account creation

AccountMapper.ToEntity() was only mapping Id and AccountName, silently dropping
the SymmetricKeys and AsymmetricKeys collections. Added ToAccountEntity() and
ToModel() overloads to SymmetricKeyMapper and AsymmetricKeyMapper (mirroring
existing user key mappers), and updated AccountMapper to map key collections.

Updated Plans/AccountKeyUsability.md to reflect current state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Wire block ID assignment into save path

ensure_block_ids() runs before save_current() writes to disk.
It parses the buffer, computes missing block IDs via
compute_block_id_assignments(), and inserts them into the rope
in reverse order (preserving char offsets). Insertions are
grouped as a single undo step.

The buffer shows the IDs immediately after save. The disk writer
gets content with IDs included. The indexer picks them up via
the normal file watcher pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(costs): use apiClient instead of raw axios to reach Railway backend

Raw axios with relative /api/admin/costs URL was hitting Vercel (which
serves index.html for GET but returns 405 for POST/PUT/DELETE).
apiClient points to the correct Railway backend URL and auto-injects
the Bearer token via interceptor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
